source_jobs.title: Software Engineer - MeritFirst
source_jobs.description:
About MeritFirst

At MeritFirst, we’re on a mission to revolutionize how companies identify and evaluate talent, regardless of background. By rejecting outdated, unreliable proxies for capability and embracing objective, merit-based assessments, we are rebuilding the meritocratic infrastructure of opportunity from the ground up.



Role Overview

As a Software Engineer at MeritFirst, you’ll be responsible for developing, testing, and deploying software solutions that meet the needs of our users. You’ll take features from initial idea to live product. Your work will power real‑time assessments, AI workflows, and tools that hiring managers rely on to build excellent teams. You will help transform the way organizations discover and empower talent across industries, setting a new standard for merit-based hiring.



What You’ll Do

 - Own features end‑to‑end: work with our CEO, company partners, and candidate users to define scope and sketch UX.

 - Build with Next.js + TypeScript, wire up data in Postgres / Drizzle, ship on Vercel, and iterate.

 - Shape product direction: propose experiments, cut scope intelligently, and use data and feedback to guide future decisions.

 - Keep things fast and reliable: instrument analytics / telemetry, write tests, review PRs, and improve performance and developer ergonomics.



Qualifications

Must-Haves

 - Meaningful experience building production web apps withTypeScript / React / Next.js and Postgres (impact > exact years).

 - Ability to design clean DB schemas, write performant queries, and reason about cost.

 - Product mindset: enjoy clarifying the core problem and delighting users through UX polish.

 - Crisp communication: written and async, and a bias toward action and ownership over waiting for direction.



Nice‑to‑Haves

 - Experience with real‑time video or proctoring technology, AI/LLM pipelines, or large‑scale assessment platforms.

 - Passion for meritocracy and building products that broaden access to opportunity.



Why Join Us?

 - Autonomy: rapid growth with outsized ownership and scope.

 - Modern stack: TypeScript, Next.js, Postgres, Drizzle, Vercel, LLMs.

 - Meritocracy: We focus on real capabilities, not credentials. You’ll be assessed based on your skills and ability to execute, not the name of your school or employer.

 - Innovation: Work on cutting-edge projects that challenge the status quo and empower people to succeed.

 - Backed by the best: We’re funded by 8VC and Slow Ventures, with support from world-class builders like Joe Lonsdale and Sam Lessin who’ve helped scale some of the most influential tech companies of the last decade.



Our Process

We practice what we preach. Core to our thesis is that resumes, school names, and pedigree are poor proxies for capability. We want to see what you can actually do.

We have designed a hiring process that gives everyone a fair shot to showcase their skills through objective work samples.



The Steps:

 1. Assessment 1 (The Gateway): Linked here https://www.meritfirst.us/candidate/tests/0a446564-7943-4d3f-85cb-20617549f8a9. Want to jump the line? You can start this right now. We prioritize candidates who have data on the board.

 2. Team Screen: A conversation with a member of our team to discuss your background, our company, and the results of your first assessment.

 3. Assessment 2 (The Deep Dive).

 4. Work Sample Debrief: A deep-dive interview where we discuss your approach to Assessment 2.

 5. Final Sync: A final conversation with one or more members of our team.



Important Note on Location:

We are an in-person team based in Austin, TX. We believe the speed of iteration required at this stage happens best when we are in the same room.



The "No Dead Ends" Guarantee:

Even if this specific role isn't the right fit - or if the location doesn't work out - your effort won't be wasted. By completing our assessments, you will create a verified profile in the MeritFirst Talent Network. This allows you to showcase

source_jobs.title: Product Designer - Thorin
source_jobs.description:
Title: Product Designer

Location: San Francisco
Company: Thorin
http://www.thorin.comStage: Seed | Incubated at 8VC



About Thorin



Thorin is an applied AI company born out of 8VC’s Build program, focused on redefining how work gets done inside modern organizations. We build proactive, long-horizon AI agents that continuously observe work across Slack, email, meetings, and documents—and take action so nothing falls through the cracks.



Unlike traditional AI tools that wait for prompts or rely on structured systems, Thorin creates a real-time digital twin of how a business actually operates, enabling automation, coordination, and insight over months and years. Backed by 8VC and built with product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is building foundational infrastructure for agentic, AI-powered productivity.


With backing and product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is 8VC’s latest incubation, following the success of Affinity, Addepar, Saronic, and other unicorns.




About the Role



We’re hiring a Product Designer to help define and shape the core user experience of Thorin’s AI-powered product. You’ll play a critical role in translating complex AI capabilities into intuitive, elegant workflows that feel natural and valuable to enterprise users.

This role sits at the intersection of product strategy, design craft, and technical collaboration. You’ll take ideas from early concepts through execution, helping ensure Thorin’s product is not only powerful under the hood, but clear, usable, and delightful on the surface.




What You’ll Do



 - Design intuitive, end-to-end product experiences that translate complex AI capabilities into clear, usable workflows.

 - Partner closely with product and engineering to shape features from early concept through launch.

 - Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity designs to explore ideas and guide implementation.

 - Gather and synthesize user feedback to iterate quickly and improve core experiences.

 - Help evolve Thorin’s design system and uphold a high bar for design quality.




What You’ll Bring



 - 4+ years of experience as a Product Designer (or equivalent), ideally in SaaS or enterprise products.

 - Experience designing AI-powered products.

 - Familiarity with enterprise workflows, productivity tools, or developer-facing products.

 - Strong product thinking — you can connect user needs, business goals, and technical constraints.

 - Excellent visual, interaction, and UX design skills, with a portfolio that demonstrates end-to-end ownership.

 - Experience collaborating closely with engineering and product in fast-moving teams.

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and shaping problems from scratch in a 0→1 environment.
   



About the Team & Why Thorin



 - Be among the first design hires and shape foundational AI direction.

 - Work closely with a small, highly driven founding team, design partners, and directly with Joe Lonsdale, who remains deeply involved in product vision, strategy, and hiring

 - Operate with high agency, rapid iteration, and direct customer exposure from day one

 - Help define how a category-defining AI company goes to market from first principles

 - Build with the backing of one of the strongest early-stage venture platforms in the industry

source_jobs.title: AI Researcher - Thorin
source_jobs.description:
Title: AI Researcher

Location: San Francisco
Company: Thorin
http://www.thorin.comStage: Seed | Incubated at 8VC



About Thorin



Thorin is an applied AI company born out of 8VC’s Build program, focused on redefining how work gets done inside modern organizations. We build proactive, long-horizon AI agents that continuously observe work across Slack, email, meetings, and documents—and take action so nothing falls through the cracks.



Unlike traditional AI tools that wait for prompts or rely on structured systems, Thorin creates a real-time digital twin of how a business actually operates, enabling automation, coordination, and insight over months and years. Backed by 8VC and built with product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is building foundational infrastructure for agentic, AI-powered productivity.


With backing and product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is 8VC’s latest incubation, following the success of Affinity, Addepar, Saronic, and other unicorns.



About the Role



We’re hiring an AI Researcher who will help define and deliver the core research work that powers Thorin’s AI differentiator. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning research, applied model development, and product impact, advancing models and methods that enable deeper understanding and automation of enterprise workflows.



This role blends rigorous research thinking with practical implementation — moving ideas from concept through experimentation and into usable components that shape Thorin’s product and capabilities.



What You’ll Do



Research & Innovation

 - Develop novel machine learning research grounded in real-world product needs.

 - Explore new model architectures, training paradigms, and evaluation techniques tailored to organizational workflow understanding and automation.

Model Development & Evaluation

 - Design, implement, and test ML / AI methods that improve model performance on core tasks.

 - Build and refine datasets, metrics, and evaluations that drive research progress and product impact.

Collaborate Across Teams

 - Partner with engineering, product, and data teams to translate research insights into production-ready algorithms and features.

 - Present research findings internally to inform strategic decisions and technical directions.

Prototyping & Deployment

 - Build prototypes and proof-of-concept models that demonstrate feasibility and impact.

 - Support iteration toward scalable, reliable systems suitable for integration with Thorin’s stack.

Stay at the Frontier

 - Track and contribute to the latest developments in ML, deep learning, and AI systems.

 - Represent Thorin’s research in the community when appropriate (writing, talks, shared benchmarks).



What You’ll Bring



 - Advanced degree (Master’s/Ph.D.) in Machine Learning, Computer Science, Statistics, or a related field — or equivalent research experience.

 - Strong foundation in deep learning, probabilistic models, and large-scale AI systems.

 - Experience with modeling frameworks such as PyTorch or TensorFlow.

 - Ability to bridge research and product engineering — designing experiments and implementing them with reliability in mind.

 - Creative problem-solving skills and a track record of working in ambiguous, early-stage environments.

 - Excellent communication — you can explain difficult concepts clearly and make data-driven recommendations.



Bonus Points For



 - Publications or research contributions in relevant areas (NLP, representation learning, self-supervised learning, or workflow automation).

 - Experience with LLMs, retrieval systems, or structured reasoning models.

 - Prior work in enterprise AI, productivity automation, or human-machine collaboration.

 - Familiarity with methods for fine-tuning, evaluation, or scaling of foundation models.



About the Team & Why Thorin



 - Be among the first research hires and shape foundational AI direction.

 - Work closely with a small, highly driven founding team, desi

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, New Grad
source_jobs.description:
SOFTWARE ENGINEER, NEW GRAD

Location: San Francisco
Company: Thorin
Stage: Seed | Incubated at 8VC




ABOUT THORIN

Thorin is an Applied AI company born out of 8VC’s Build program, focused on redefining how work gets done inside modern organizations.

We build proactive, long-horizon AI agents that continuously observe work across Slack, email, meetings, and documents—and take action so nothing falls through the cracks.

Unlike traditional AI tools that wait to be prompted, Thorin creates a real-time digital twin of how a business actually operates. This enables automation, coordination, and insight over months and years—not just single tasks.

Backed by 8VC and built with product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is building foundational infrastructure for agentic, AI-powered productivity. Thorin follows 8VC incubations such as Affinity, Addepar, and Saronic.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a New Grad Software Engineer to help build and scale Thorin’s core product. This role is designed for strong generalists early in their careers who want to work across the stack, learn quickly, and ship real features used by real customers.

You’ll work closely with senior engineers and the founding team to build user-facing functionality, backend systems, and AI-powered workflows. This is a hands-on role with meaningful ownership, mentorship, and exposure to product and customers from day one.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Build and ship product features used by customers, from idea to production

 - Work across the stack, including the core AI system

 - Collaborate closely with designers, product, and founders to iterate quickly

 - Learn how to build and scale software in a zero-to-one startup environment
   
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - New grad (or recent graduate) in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field

 - Strong programming fundamentals and comfort working in at least one modern language

 - Interest in full-stack development (frontend + backend)

 - Product-minded: you care about usability, quality, and real-world impact

 - Comfortable learning quickly, taking feedback, and working in ambiguity

 - Excited about startups, ownership, and building from scratch
   
   


NICE TO HAVE (NOT REQUIRED)

 - Familiarity with building and evaluating AI-powered applications

 - Exposure to backend systems, APIs, or cloud infrastructure

 - Internships, side projects, or research related to software or AI
   
   


TEAM & WHY THORIN

 - Work directly with a small, highly driven founding team

 - Direct exposure to customers, product decisions, and company strategy

 - Learn from and collaborate with Joe Lonsdale, who remains deeply involved in product and hiring

 - High-ownership environment with real responsibility early in your career

 - Backed by one of the strongest early-stage venture platforms in the industry

source_jobs.title: Head of Communications & Brand Marketing
source_jobs.description:
HEAD OF COMMUNICATIONS & BRAND MARKETING

Remote | Full-Time | Noho Labs




ABOUT NOHO LABS

Noho Labs is building a more trusted, clinically rigorous way for patients to access peptide-based care.

The peptide market is growing quickly, but it is also confusing, fragmented, and full of noise. Noho combines telehealth, clinical oversight, research, education, and modern patient experience to bring more clarity, trust, and structure to an emerging category of medicine.

We are building a company that can help define the next generation of peptide, longevity, and health optimization care.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a communications and brand marketing leader who can be the voice of Noho Labs.

This person will own how Noho shows up in the world: our messaging, PR, social presence, executive voice, influencer relationships, and brand storytelling. You’ll translate complex clinical, scientific, and health topics into language that feels clear, credible, and compelling.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


OWN THE NOHO LABS NARRATIVE

 - Build and refine Noho’s core messaging

 - Translate topics like peptides, longevity, hormone health, recovery, compounding, telehealth, and clinical care into language people understand and trust.

 - Create messaging foundations including positioning, boilerplate, founder narrative, FAQs, patient education, launch messaging, and internal talking points.

 - Write across formats: website copy, blog posts, patient education, email, social, press materials, founder posts, creator briefs, launch copy, and recruiting content.


LEAD COMMUNICATIONS AND PR

 - Build and execute Noho’s media strategy across health, wellness, business, technology, pharma, longevity, and lifestyle media.

 - Identify and build relationships with the right journalists, podcasts, newsletters, creators, and industry voices.

 - Write pitches, manage inbound and outbound media opportunities, prepare executives for interviews, and create briefing materials.

 - Support launches, partnerships, research updates, clinical milestones, funding announcements, and sensitive communications.


BUILD EXECUTIVE THOUGHT LEADERSHIP

 - Shape the voice of Noho’s founder and leadership team across LinkedIn, X, podcasts, interviews, essays, panels, and speaking opportunities.

 - Turn leadership ideas into sharp, high-signal content that builds credibility.

 - Identify timely topics where Noho should have a point of view across peptide medicine, patient safety, telehealth, compounding, longevity, and personalized care.


BUILD SOCIAL AND CULTURAL RELEVANCE

 - Own Noho’s social presence across relevant channels.

 - Develop content that educates, builds trust, and makes Noho feel like a category-defining brand.

 - Monitor trends across longevity, peptides, wellness, fitness, hormone health, GLP-1s, health tech, and patient communities.

 - Identify timely opportunities to join the conversation and know when not to.


LEAD INFLUENCER AND CREATOR STRATEGY

 - Build Noho’s influencer and creator strategy across health, fitness, longevity, wellness, medicine, and lifestyle.

 - Identify credible creators, practitioners, educators, podcasters, and community voices who can shape patient understanding.

 - Manage outreach, briefs, approvals, content quality, and performance tracking.

 - Create partnerships that feel authentic, educational, and brand-aligned.


BUILD THE SYSTEM

 - Create repeatable processes for content planning, media tracking, influencer outreach, social publishing, executive communications, and reporting.

 - Build the communications calendar and ensure company moments are planned and executed well.

 - Bring structure without slowing the company down.




WHO YOU ARE

 - You have 5+ years of experience across communications, PR, brand marketing, content strategy, social media, or related fields.

 - You are an exceptional writer with strong taste, precision, and voice.

 - You have experience building or sharpening a company narra

source_jobs.title: AI Engineer - Thorin
source_jobs.description:
Title: AI Engineer

Location: San Francisco
Company: Thorin
http://www.thorin.comStage: Seed | Incubated at 8VC



About Thorin



Thorin is an applied AI company born out of 8VC’s Build program, focused on redefining how work gets done inside modern organizations. We build proactive, long-horizon AI agents that continuously observe work across Slack, email, meetings, and documents—and take action so nothing falls through the cracks.



Unlike traditional AI tools that wait for prompts or rely on structured systems, Thorin creates a real-time digital twin of how a business actually operates, enabling automation, coordination, and insight over months and years. Backed by 8VC and built with product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is building foundational infrastructure for agentic, AI-powered productivity.


With backing and product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is 8VC’s latest incubation, following the success of Affinity, Addepar, Saronic, and other unicorns.



About the Role



We’re looking for a Software Engineer (AI Engineer) to build the foundation of Thorin’s user-facing product. This role is ideal for full-stack generalists who love to ship, own features end-to-end, and bring product ideas to life with speed and polish.



You’ll work across the stack — building frontend interfaces, backend logic, and everything in between.



This role is high-ownership and high-agency.



What You'll Do



 - Build and ship core product features from concept to production

 - Own frontend and backend logic across key workflows

 - Set quality and velocity benchmarks for a high-performance engineering team

 - Help shape product direction through close feedback loops with users and stakeholders

 - Move quickly and thoughtfully in a zero-to-one environment



What You Bring



 - Full-stack experience shipping production-grade SaaS or AI-native applications

 - Strong product intuition — you care about the user and sweat the details

 - Proven ability to work across the stack: frontend frameworks (React), backend systems (Bun, TypeScript), and APIs

 - Comfortable with ambiguity, fast iteration, and early-stage startup dynamics

 - Passionate about building software that’s useful, fast, and delightful

 - Bonus: Experience integrating LLMs or building AI-driven workflows



About the Team & Why Thorin



 - Work closely with a small, highly driven founding team, design partners, and directly with Joe Lonsdale, who remains deeply involved in product vision, strategy, and hiring

 - Operate with high agency, rapid iteration, and direct customer exposure from day one

 - Help define how a category-defining AI company goes to market from first principles

 - Build with the backing of one of the strongest early-stage venture platforms in the industry

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer - Thorin
source_jobs.description:
TITLE: PRODUCT ENGINEER

Location: San Francisco
Company: Thorin
Stage: Seed



About Thorin

Most enterprise software still depends on people to push work forward, and things constantly fall through the cracks. Thorin is building autonomous AI agents that understand the full context of your organization, reading across Slack, email, docs, and other work surfaces to proactively keep things on track without anyone having to remember to follow up.

The company was cofounded by Markus Zirn, co-founder of Workato, and Joe Lonsdale, co-founder of Palantir. We have strong venture backing and are focused on what AI can fundamentally change about how companies operate, not just incremental improvements to existing workflows.



About the Role

We're building an AI product for enterprise customers. We need someone who can own large pieces of it end to end.

You should be a strong frontend engineer (TypeScript, React) who ships fast and writes clean code. You should have real experience building AI-powered products at work or at home, but thinking hard about what new interaction patterns are possible when models get better. You should have good product taste and often-updated opinions about what to build, not just how.



What We’re Looking For

 - 1+ year(s) experience building and shipping user-facing products in TypeScript/React

 - Has built with AI extensively at work or with side projects

 - Can look at a complex AI capability and figure out how to make it simple and useful for a non-technical user

 - Fast. High standards for code and UX

 - Comfortable in ambiguity at an early-stage company
   
   

About the Team & Why Thorin

 - Be among the first engineering hires and help shape the core product and technical foundation.

 - Work closely with a small, highly driven founding team and design partners

 - Operate with high agency, rapid iteration, and direct customer exposure from day one.

 - Help define how a category-defining AI company builds and ships product from first principles.

 - Build with the backing of one of the strongest early-stage venture platforms in the industry.

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Manager (Applied AI) - Thorin
source_jobs.description:
Title: Founding Product Manager

Location: San Francisco

Company: Thorin https://www.thorin.com

Stage: Seed | Incubated at 8VC



About Thorin



Thorin is an applied AI company born out of 8VC’s Build program, focused on redefining how work gets done inside modern organizations. We build proactive, long-horizon AI agents that continuously observe work across Slack, email, meetings, and documents—and take action so nothing falls through the cracks.



Unlike traditional AI tools that wait for prompts or rely on structured systems, Thorin creates a real-time digital twin of how a business actually operates, enabling automation, coordination, and insight over months and years. Backed by 8VC and built with product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is building foundational infrastructure for agentic, AI-powered productivity.


With backing and product vision from Joe Lonsdale, Thorin is 8VC’s latest incubation, following the success of Affinity, Addepar, Saronic, and other unicorns.



About the Role



We’re hiring a Founding Product Manager to help define Thorin’s product from first principles. This is a core founding role, sitting at the intersection of AI systems, enterprise infrastructure, and real-world business operations.



You will own product direction end-to-end: shaping Thorin’s primitives, deciding what the system should understand and automate, and ensuring that powerful agentic capabilities are exposed through products that enterprise leaders can trust, adopt, and scale.



This role is not only about backlog management. It’s about deciding what Thorin should become.



What You’ll Do



 - Own Thorin’s product vision and roadmap across visibility, process modeling, guardrails, and agentic execution

 - Define the core abstractions of the Thorin platform — what a “process,” “policy,” “metric,” and “agent” mean in a living enterprise system

 - Partner deeply with AI Engineers to shape agent behavior, evaluation frameworks, reliability thresholds, and iteration loops

 - Work hand-in-hand with Infrastructure leadership to ensure product decisions meet enterprise-grade security, compliance, and scalability requirements

 - Collaborate with Product Engineers to design and ship intuitive user experiences on top of complex AI systems

 - Lead customer discovery with design partners and early enterprise adopters, translating messy operational reality into clear product direction



What You Bring



 - 5+ years of experience building and owning complex software products, ideally in enterprise, platform, or infrastructure-adjacent domains

 - Proven ability to operate as a founding or early-stage PM, defining product direction in the absence of precedent

 - Strong technical fluency — you can reason about AI systems, distributed infrastructure, and product tradeoffs without needing abstraction layers.

 - CS degree strongly preferred

 - Experience working closely with engineers on systems where reliability, trust, and correctness matter

 - Exceptional product judgment — you can turn ambiguous, high-stakes problems into crisp product decisions

 - Comfort with uncomfortable ambiguity — you enjoy operating without playbooks

 - Clear, structured project management and communication skills — you can align engineers, customers, and leadership around a shared product vision



Bonus:



 - Experience with AI-native, agentic, or automation-heavy products

 - Familiarity with enterprise workflows

 - Background in process modeling, operational analytics, or enterprise systems of record

 - Prior experience selling or deploying products into large enterprises



About the Team & Why Thorin



 - Work closely with a small, highly driven founding team, design partners, and directly with Joe Lonsdale, who remains deeply involved in product vision, strategy, and hiring

 - Operate with high agency, rapid iteration, and direct customer exposure from day one

 - Help define how a category-defining AI company goes to market from firs

source_jobs.title: IT Administrator
source_jobs.description:
IT ADMINISTRATOR WITH 8VC PORTFOLIO COMPANY (ONSITE, DALLAS, TX)




ROLE OVERVIEW

We are seeking a skilled IT Administrator to support our U.S. team as we expand our operations. In this role, you will oversee all aspects of our information technology functions, ensuring data security, regulatory compliance, and the effective management of all IT domains. This is a full-time, onsite position based in Dallas, TX.




ABOUT US

We are a U.S. defense company specializing in delivering advanced hardware solutions to address strategic challenges facing the United States and its allies. Our work is conducted in close partnership with government stakeholders, built on a culture of accountability, discretion, and execution.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Manage day-to-day IT operations to ensure secure, reliable, efficient, and compliant office systems.

 - Coordinate IT activities with internal teams, external vendors, and third-party assessors.

 - Maintain and enhance our cybersecurity posture by implementing and maintaining controls aligned to NIST (800-171/53), ITAR, and CMMC Level 2 compliance standards.

 - Ensure ongoing compliance: keep the organization audit-ready, manage SSPs, and address cybersecurity gaps promptly.

 - Administer, expand, and secure U.S. systems, identities, endpoints, and access rights.

 - Enforce least privilege principles; onboard and manage user access, and maintain hardened systems.

 - Monitor security tasks, track deadlines, and deliver compliance objectives.

 - Develop and update IT policies, processes, and documentation to support company growth.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Maintain ongoing security, compliance, and audit readiness for all IT systems.

 - Uphold a strong, defensible security posture for U.S. government work.

 - Ensure all systems and data adhere to federal cybersecurity standards.




WHAT YOU BRING

 - Exceptional organizational and ownership skills with strong attention to detail.

 - Sound judgment and reliability in a fast-paced, high-accountability environment.

 - Clear communicator with both technical and non-technical senior stakeholders.

 - Experience with or supporting DoD/U.S. government regulated environments.

 - 5+ years in IT support, systems administration, or similar roles.

 - Ability to work onsite in Dallas, TX.



Preferred:

 - Active U.S. Government Security Clearance.




WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This position is fundamental to maintaining secure, compliant operations with U.S. government partners. Your expertise will help build the infrastructure and safeguards that empower our team to perform impactful work of national importance in a high-trust environment.

notes: Multiple job postings for Thorin, an applied AI company, and other 8VC portfolio companies.
